violations.text: Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (floor mixer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC took out of service to be washed and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025

The doors to the outside, located in the front dinning area and back kitchen area, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025
Single service items were observed stored on the ground in the storage closet. PIC moved items. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the storage shelf have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the back storage area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025

The following were observed in poor repair: -holes in wall and ceiling in storage area, -leak in ceiling above 3 bay sink. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2025

The following food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. -can opener, -food chopper, -floor mixer, -ice machine. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC placed in 3 bay sink to be washed and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2025

Rice and garlic was observed held between 53-65 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC placed in freezer to cool. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2025

Various items were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2025
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2025

Chlorine sanitizer solution was observed in excess of 200 parts per million (ppm). A chlorine sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC refilled sink. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2025

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2025

The presence of rodents are not controlled as evidenced by large droppings and large openings. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. PIC called for PCS during inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2025
Various food items were observed stored on the floor in the kitchen and walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2025
Various food items were observed stored in non-food grade plastic grocery bags. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2025
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2025
Equipment was observed stored on the floor in the kitchen area. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2025
Blender with lid closed was observed with water residue. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2025
Single service items were observed stored on the floor in the bar area. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2025
Food processor lid located in the kitchen area was not in good repair (cracked). PIC discarded. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2025

The following non-food contact surfaces have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris:-2 door cooler units,-freezer units,-prep table shelves,-blender base,-storage shelves. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2025
Plumbing under 3 bay sink was observed in poor repair (leak). New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2025

Various holes were observed in the walls in storage closet, kitchen area, and bar area and a hole in the ceiling in the storage closest. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the storage closet area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2025
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. PIC informed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/30/2023
Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 11/16/2023
Inspector observed small flies on onions located in a bowl. PIC washed onions and covered them during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/16/2023

The chlorine concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was (200) parts per million (ppm). The concentration of chlorine in the sanitizing solution must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC informed and diluted solution with water. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/16/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener and ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. Food processor observed in poor repair. (cracked, piece(s) missing) The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Equipment taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized/other unit removed from establishment. New Violation. Correct By: 11/16/2023

Various sauces, curries, and prepared foods were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 11/16/2023

The presence of pests is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings, flies, a dead rodent in trap. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 11/16/2023

Onions, raw chicken, etc. were observed stored on the floor in the kitchen and walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Foods relocated during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/16/2023
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 11/16/2023

The cutting boards in the kitchen are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2023
The prep unit located in the kitchen was not in good repair. (Ambient 54 degrees F.) No TCS foods observed in unit @ time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 11/16/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the rice cooker, interior of prep unit, storage shelves have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2023
The plumbing to the mop sink is in poor repair. Sink observed clogged. New Violation. Correct By: 11/17/2023

The floors, walls, and equipment throughout the kitchen area have an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2023

An accumulation of unnecessary and/or non-functional items were observed in the storage room, wait staff service area, and mop sink. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 11/27/2023
